_about: Environment variables to paste into Netlify → Site → Environment variables. Grouped by what's required now vs deferred. VITE_-prefixed vars are baked into the frontend at build time; everything else is server-side and read by the API function at runtime.

TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN.description: Turso auth token with read + write.
TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN.where_to_get: Turso dashboard → your database → Tokens → Create token.

CLERK_SECRET_KEY.description: Clerk server-side secret. Validates sessions on every /api request.
CLERK_SECRET_KEY.where_to_get: Clerk dashboard → API Keys → Secret key.

VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY.description: Clerk publishable key, browser-safe. Must be VITE_-prefixed to be baked into the frontend bundle.
VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY.where_to_get: Clerk dashboard → API Keys → Publishable key.

DEEPSEEK_API_KEY.description: Powers AI repo analysis (category, tags, summary, App Killer flag).

GEMINI_API_KEY.description: Powers vector embeddings for semantic search / recommendations.
GEMINI_API_KEY.where_to_get: ai.google.dev → Get API key (Google AI Studio).

GITHUB_TOKEN.description: GitHub Personal Access Token. Raises ingest rate limit from 60 to 5000 req/hr. Public-repo read scope is enough.

APP_URL.description: Canonical public URL of the deployed site. Used for self-referential links shown in the UI.

ADMIN_USER_IDS.description: Comma-separated Clerk user ids allowed to curate the preloaded App Killers library. Leave blank until you've signed in once and run `npm run tenants` (or checked Clerk dashboard) to find your user_... id.

deferred_phase_2_billing._note: These are placeholders for the Stripe wiring that comes in Phase 2 billing. Leave unset until that ships — the app does not read them yet.

do_not_set_on_netlify.PORT: Netlify manages the listening port internally — leave unset.
do_not_set_on_netlify.NODE_ENV: Netlify sets this to 'production' automatically during builds and function runtime.
do_not_set_on_netlify.URL: Auto-injected by Netlify (the canonical site URL). If you'd rather drive APP_URL from this at runtime, you can — but setting APP_URL explicitly is clearer.
